figures.caption: Fig. 2: An overview of the proposed MMCD architecture. It contains three core components: a modal-specific feature encoder based on a pseudo-siamese feature extractor, a spatio-temporal common feature modeling module, and a multimodal feature fusion module guided by change priors.
Fig. 3: Details of the Modal-specific feature Extractor.
TABLE III: Evaluation of building damage result accuracy of different methods on the BRIGHT dataset (%).

TABLE IV: Evaluation of MMCD results on the Delta-SN6 dataset (%). BG, AB, AR, AW, DB, DR and DW refer to background, added building, added road, added water, disappeared building, disappeared road and disappeared water, respectively.
Fig. 4: Extraction of cross-modal common features with spatio-temporal correlations.
Fig. 5: Workflow for fusing modality-specific and common features guided by change priors.
Fig. 6: Results of different methods in the testing areas (a)-(d) on the Wuhan-Het dataset.
Fig. 12: Visualization of specific features, common features, and the fused change feature.
Fig. 8: Results among different methods in the testing areas (a)-(d) on the Delta-SN6 dataset.
Fig. 1: The Delta-SN6 dataset provides multimodal and multi-temporal remote sensing images along with ground truth labels, designed for MMCD task.
Fig. 7: Results among different methods in the testing areas (a)-(d) on the BRIGHT dataset.
TABLE II: Evaluation of MMCD results on the Wuhan-Het dataset (%). The best and second-best results are highlighted and underlined, respectively.
